pubmed_links: [Gai et al. (2011) Rare structural variation of synapse and neurotransmission genes in autism](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/21358714/); [Gonzalez-Mantilla et al. (2016) A Cross-Disorder Method to Identify Novel Candidate Genes for Developmental Brain Disorders](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/26817790/)
summary: LRBA holds a SFARI Gene Score of 2 (Strong Candidate) for autism, with copy-number loss variants identified in ASD probands from multiple large cohorts and loss-of-function variants flagged across cross-disorder brain developmental disorder analyses; LRBA deficiency is also known to cause primary immunodeficiency with neurological manifestations. (emerging evidence)
